message:
chore(node): add acceptance criteria to open beads issue tracker entries

Enrich four open/closed bead entries (bd-1icm7, bd-1yw0w, bd-1z2in,
bd-2286h) with explicit acceptance_criteria fields so downstream agents
and operators have unambiguous pass/fail definitions without needing to
re-derive them from notes or titles. No behavioral changes; metadata
only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Verify that JSON output (--json flag) includes pre_flight_verdict object with per-extension results.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T01:09:26.082633416Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T14:21:46.028849935Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-1icm7","title":"Track 2a: Add pre-execution trust gate to run command","design":"EDGE CASES TO HANDLE:
1. Trust registry file doesn't exist yet: treat as empty registry, not an error. In strict mode, warn that no trust data available but don't block (first-run scenario).
2. Package.json has no dependencies: pre-flight passes trivially with empty result set.
3. Package.json doesn't exist: skip dependency scanning, warn in logs.
4. Registry file is corrupt (invalid JSON): fail the pre-flight check in strict/balanced, warn in legacy-risky.
5. Extension ID in package.json doesn't match any trust card: log as untracked, don't block unless policy requires cards for all deps.

DESIGN PATTERN: Use a PreFlightVerdict enum:
- Passed { checked: usize, warnings: Vec<String> }
- Blocked { reason: String, violations: Vec<TrustViolation> }
- Skipped { reason: String }

The verdict flows into the execution receipt (bd-3ewxi) and JSON output.","acceptance_criteria":"1. strict mode blocks execution when ANY dependency has revoked trust card
2. balanced mode warns on missing trust cards but blocks on revoked
3. legacy-risky mode logs all trust findings but never blocks
4. Pre-flight verdict appears in JSON output when --json flag used
5. Pre-flight check loads registry from .franken-node/state/trust-card-registry.v1.json
6. Decision receipt generated with per-extension trust results
7. Execution blocked when quarantined extension detected (strict/balanced)
8. All tests pass with fixture trust registry containing mixed card states","notes":"TEST DETAIL: Each test must use tracing_subscriber::fmt::TestWriter for structured log capture. Assert on specific tracing event fields (trust_card_id, revocation_status, policy_verdict). Integration tests should use tempdir with a fixture trust-card-registry.v1.json containing: 1 clean extension, 1 revoked extension, 1 quarantined extension, 1 extension with stale revocation check. Verify that JSON output (--json flag) includes pre_flight_verdict object with per-extension results.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T01:09:26.082633416Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T14:24:38.129692405Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1id0","title":"[10.15] Publish tri-kernel ownership contract (`franken_engine`, `asupersync`, `franken_node`) with explicit interface boundaries.","description":"## Why This Exists
The three-kernel architecture partitions franken_node into three responsibility planes: franken_engine (execution kernel), asupersync (correctness/control kernel), and franken_node (product kernel). Without an explicit, machine-enforceable ownership contract, module boundaries erode — execution logic leaks into the product plane, correctness invariants get bypassed, and the 10 Hard Runtime Invariants from Section 8.5 lose their structural anchor. This bead publishes the foundational tri-kernel ownership contract that every subsequent 10.15 bead depends on: it names which kernel owns each capability (Cx propagation, region lifecycle, cancellation protocol, obligation tracking, scheduler lanes, remote effects, epoch management, evidence ledger, deterministic verification, and authority gating), defines the interface boundaries between them, and makes boundary violations deterministic CI failures.

## What This Must Do
1. Author `docs/architecture/tri_kernel_ownership_contract.md` that:
   - Names the three kernels (franken_engine, asupersync, franken_node) and their responsibility planes (execution, correctness/control, product).
   - Maps each of the 10 Hard Runtime Invariants (8.5) to its canonical owner kernel.
   - Defines the permitted inter-kernel API surface: which types, traits, and function signatures may cross boundaries.
   - Enumerates any current exceptions with time-bounded migration deadlines and signed waiver requirements.
2. Implement `tests/conformance/ownership_boundary_checks.rs` that:
   - Parses the crate dependency graph and module structure under `crates/franken-node/src/`.
   - Asserts that no module in the product plane directly invokes correctness-kernel internals bypassing the defined interface.
   - Asserts that no execution-kernel primitive is re-implemented in the product plane.
   - Fails deterministically with a structured error listing the violating module path, the boundary crossed, and the contract clause violated.
3. Generate `artifacts/10.15/ownership_boundary_report.json` containing:
   - Per-module ownership classification.
   - Boundary crossing audit results (pass/fail per boundary).
   - Exception inventory with waiver status and expiry dates.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Contract names owners for execution, correctness, and product planes; boundary violations have deterministic CI failures; exceptions require signed waiver artifact.
- The contract document is parseable by CI tooling (structured frontmatter with kernel-to-capability mapping).
- Conformance test covers all modules under `crates/franken-node/src/connector/`, `crates/franken-node/src/conformance/`, and `crates/franken-node/src/`.
- Any unsigned or expired waiver causes a hard CI failure, not a warning.
- The ownership report JSON schema is stable and consumed by the section gate (bd-20eg).

## Testing & Logging Requirements
- **Unit tests**: Validate contract document parsing, waiver expiry logic, and boundary classification for synthetic module graphs.
- **Integration tests**: Run ownership_boundary_checks.rs against the real crate tree and assert zero violations (or only waivered ones).
- **Conformance tests**: Inject a synthetic boundary violation (e.g., product module importing correctness-kernel internal) and assert deterministic failure with correct error code.
- **Adversarial tests**: Attempt to bypass the gate with an expired waiver, an unsigned waiver, and a waiver for a non-existent module.
- **Structured logs**: Event codes `OWN-001` (boundary check pass), `OWN-002` (boundary violation detected), `OWN-003` (waiver applied), `OWN-004` (waiver expired/invalid). All entries include trace correlation ID and module path.

## Expected Artifacts
- `docs/architecture/tri_kernel_ownership_contract.md`
- `tests/conformance/ownership_boundary_checks.rs`
- `artifacts/10.15/ownership_boundary_report.json`
- `artifacts/section_10_15/bd-1id0/verification_evidence.json`
- `artifacts/section_10_15/bd-1id0/verification_summary.md`

## Dependencies
- **Upstream**: bd-cda (Execution Normalization Contract), bd-5rh (10.14 FrankenSQLite track), bd-1ow (10.1 Charter + Split Governance)
- **Downstream**: bd-2177 (workflow inventory), bd-33kj (claim-language policy), bd-20eg (section gate)","acceptance_criteria":"- Contract names owners for execution, correctness, and product planes; boundary violations have deterministic CI failures; exceptions require signed waiver artifact.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"NavyPeak","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:59.474000123Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T02:35:54.313802935Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T02:35:54.313774653Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-15","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1id0","depends_on_id":"bd-1ow","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:04Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1id0","depends_on_id":"bd-5rh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:04Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1id0","depends_on_id":"bd-cda","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:04Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Only 4 duplicate_mod warnings remain (structural, require module reorganization). Clippy exits 0, all 7400+ tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1ylb","title":"Fresh-eyes audit: randomly selected franken-node module trace and bug-fix pass","description":"Why this exists:
The user requested a fresh-eyes audit pass that starts from randomly selected code files, traces their functionality through related modules, and fixes any concrete bugs or obvious mistakes found along the way.

What this task must do:
- pick non-reserved franken-node modules at random,
- trace execution/data flow through related imports/callers/tests,
- identify at least one concrete, defensible bug if present,
- reserve and fix only the exact file surface needed,
- validate with the lightest appropriate checks, using rch for any cargo-heavy work.

Acceptance criteria:
1. The audited module(s) and traced workflow are documented in Agent Mail / final report.
2. Any fix is scoped narrowly, justified by actual code-path analysis, and revalidated.
3. All heavy cargo work, if any, runs through rch only.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SwiftBeaver","created_at":"2026-03-11T22:46:56.972969120Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-11T23:04:05.785307686Z","closed_at":"2026-03-11T23:04:05.785281898Z","close_reason":"Landed artifact_signing fail-closed verification fix; rch full validation limited by remote timeout/queue behavior","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","fresh-eyes","self-contained"]}
-{"id":"bd-1yw0w","title":"Track 3a: Define FleetTransport trait and shared fleet state schema","notes":"API CONTRACT DETAIL: FleetTransport trait must be object-safe (no generics in methods) so it can be used as dyn FleetTransport. All methods return Result<T, FleetTransportError> where FleetTransportError is an enum: IoError, SerializationError, LockContention, StaleState, NotInitialized. The trait should include an initialize() method that creates the state directory structure if it doesn't exist. All timestamps use chrono::DateTime<Utc>.  {"id":"bd-1z3","title":"[10.2] Implement deterministic compatibility fixture runner and result canonicalizer.","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.2 — Compatibility Core

Why This Exists:
Compatibility core as strategic wedge: policy-visible behavior, divergence receipts, L1/L2 lockstep, and spec-first fixture governance.

Task Objective:
Implement deterministic compatibility fixture runner and result canonicalizer.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Implement with explicit success/failure invariants and deterministic behavior under normal, edge, and fault scenarios.
- Ensure outcomes are verifiable via automated tests and reproducible artifact outputs.

Expected Artifacts:
- Section-owned design/spec artifact at `docs/specs/section_10_2/bd-1z3_contract.md`, capturing decision rationale, invariants, and interface boundaries.
- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_2/bd-1z3/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_2/bd-1z3/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.

Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for core logic, invariants, and error handling.
- Integration/E2E tests for end-to-end control-flow behavior.
- Detailed structured logs/telemetry with stable codes and trace correlation IDs.
- Deterministic replay fixture or reproducible artifact for failure analysis.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.2] Implement deterministic compatibility fixture runner and result canonicalizer.", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.2] Implement deterministic compatibility fixture runner and result canonicalizer." must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.2] Implement deterministic compatibility fixture runner and result canonicalizer." must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[10.2] Implement deterministic compatibility fixture runner and result canonicalizer." is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.2] Implement deterministic compatibility fixture runner and result canonicalizer." prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:44.163128134Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T09:40:35.689847016Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T09:40:35.689820326Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-2","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1z3","depends_on_id":"bd-2kf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:04Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1z5a","title":"[10.17 bug] Replace verifier-economy and replay-capsule trust surrogates with canonical signed capsule verifier","description":"Why this exists:
`crates/franken-node/src/verifier_economy/mod.rs` still treats attestation signatures as basically "algorithm label + matching key + non-empty value", `verify_capsule_integrity` still only checks hash presence, and `crates/franken-node/src/connector/verifier_sdk.rs` still accepts any non-empty `signature` field. That means the verifier economy, replay capsules, and public trust scoreboard can present as independently verifiable while still admitting decorative signatures and integrity placeholders.

What this bead must do:
Replace these trust surrogates with one canonical signed-capsule verification kernel shared across verifier-economy and connector surfaces, per the ownership map. The implementation should be stronger than a basic "verify blob signature" path: canonical serialization, detached signature verification, chunked or Merkleized trace commitments for streaming replay, minimal fraud-proof witnesses, and strict binding between claim text, capsule contents, verifier identity, and replay verdict. Verified evidence should be cheap to re-check, impossible to spoof by shape, and rich enough to support public scoreboards without hand-waving.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Attestation signatures and replay capsule integrity in `verifier_economy/mod.rs` and `connector/verifier_sdk.rs` use real canonical serialization and signature/hash verification; presence-only or shape-only acceptance is removed.
2. Capsule integrity binds at least: input state, execution trace commitment, output state, expected-result commitment, verifier identity, claim/version metadata, and issuance timestamp / freshness envelope.
3. Trace commitments are chunked or Merkleized so external verifiers can validate subtraces and emit minimal fraud proofs without replaying everything from scratch.
4. Verifier reputation / public scoreboard updates consume only verified attestations and verified capsules; unverifiable evidence is rejected or explicitly quarantined, never silently scored.
5. The connector verifier SDK and verifier-economy surface share one canonical verification kernel instead of duplicating divergent local checks.
6. Adversarial fixtures cover forged same-length signatures, payload swaps under reused signatures, truncated capsules, cross-claim replay, mismatched trace commitments, and stale/freshness-window violations.
7. Add a checker that fails if signature-present / hash-present shortcuts or equivalent trust surrogates return to replacement-critical verifier paths.
8. Deliver unit tests, integration/E2E capsule replay fixtures, structured logs, benchmark evidence, and machine-readable artifacts proving external replay and trust-score updates are grounded in real verification.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Verifier-economy and connector SDK surfaces share one canonical signed-capsule verifier that binds claim metadata, trace commitments, verifier identity, freshness envelope, and expected-result commitments; signature-present and hash-present shortcuts are forbidden.
2. External verification is user-usable: capsule failures, quarantines, and trust-score decisions emit stable reason codes, fraud-proof bundles, and operator-facing summaries suitable for scoreboards and incident reports.
3. Unit tests cover canonical serialization, chunked or Merkleized subtrace verification, freshness-window handling, payload swaps, truncated capsules, mismatched commitments, and regression checks against decorative signatures.
4. Integration tests exercise connector SDK verification, replay capsule ingestion, scoreboard updates, quarantine decisions, and external re-verification using deterministic fixtures.
5. At least one deterministic operator E2E shell script under tests/e2e/ reproduces successful replay verification, forged capsule rejection, subtrace fraud-proof extraction, quarantine, and scoreboard update logging.
6. Structured logs expose stable CAPSULE_VERIFY_* and VERIFIER_SCORE_* event families with required fields: trace_id, capsule_id, verifier_id, claim_id, commitment_digest, decision, reason_code, and fraud_proof_id.
7.  {"id":"bd-21z","title":"[10.5] Implement signed decision receipt export for high-impact actions.","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.5 — Security + Policy Product Surfaces (Item 2 of 8)

Why This Exists:
Signed decision receipts are the audit backbone of the policy system. Every high-impact action (quarantine, revocation, policy change, deployment promotion) must produce a cryptographically signed receipt that captures the decision context, evidence used, action taken, and rollback path. This enables deterministic post-hoc analysis and external verification.

Task Objective:
Implement signed decision receipt export for all high-impact actions in the policy/control system.

Detailed Acceptance Criteria:
1. Decision receipt schema captures: action type, decision timestamp, evidence references (ledger entry IDs), actor identity, policy rule chain that authorized the action, confidence context, and rollback command.
2. Receipts are cryptographically signed by the acting control-plane identity.
3. Receipt export in both machine-readable (JSON) and human-readable formats.
4. Receipts are append-only and hash-chained for tamper evidence.
5. CLI surface: receipt export accessible via franken-node incident bundle and trust commands.
6. Receipts integrate with evidence ledger (10.14) and operator copilot (10.0.8).
7. High-impact action classes requiring receipts: quarantine, revocation, policy change, deployment promotion, trust-level transition.

Key Dependencies:
- Depends on 10.14 (FrankenSQLite Deep-Mined) for evidence ledger integration.
- Depends on 10.13 (FCP Deep-Mined) for authenticated control channel.
- Consumed by 10.8 (Operational Readiness) for incident bundle retention.
- Consumed by 10.17 (Radical Expansion) for verifier SDK integration.

Expected Artifacts:
- src/security/decision_receipt.rs — receipt schema, signing, export.
- CLI integration for receipt export in incident and trust commands.
- docs/specs/section_10_5/bd-21z_contract.md
- artifacts/section_10_5/bd-21z/verification_evidence.json

Testing and Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests: receipt construction, signing, hash chain integrity, schema validation.
- Integration tests: full action -> receipt generation -> export -> verification pipeline.
- E2E tests: franken-node incident bundle producing complete receipt chain.
- Adversarial tests: receipt tampering detection, replay of receipts from different contexts.
- Structured logs: DECISION_RECEIPT_GENERATED, RECEIPT_SIGNED, RECEIPT_EXPORTED, HASH_CHAIN_VERIFIED with trace IDs.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Define a SignedReceipt struct with fields: receipt_id (UUID v7), action_name (string), actor_identity (string), timestamp (RFC-3339), input_hash (SHA-256 of serialized action input), output_hash (SHA-256 of serialized action output), decision (enum: Approved | Denied | Escalated), rationale (string), and signature (Ed25519 detached signature, base64-encoded).
2. Implement sign_receipt(receipt: &Receipt, signing_key: &Ed25519PrivateKey) -> SignedReceipt that produces a deterministic canonical JSON serialization before signing (keys sorted, no optional whitespace).
3. Implement verify_receipt(signed: &SignedReceipt, public_key: &Ed25519PublicKey) -> Result<bool> that returns true only if the signature matches the canonical form.
4. High-impact actions are tagged via a #[high_impact] attribute macro or a runtime registry; any action so tagged must produce a receipt or the call returns Err.
5. Receipts must be exportable as both JSON and CBOR; round-trip fidelity test must pass (deserialize(serialize(r)) == r).
6. Provide an export_receipts(filter: ReceiptQuery) -> Vec<SignedReceipt> query API supporting time-range and action-name filters.
7. Verification: scripts/check_signed_receipt.py --json validates signature correctness on sample receipts; unit tests cover sign, verify, tamper-detection (flipped bit fails verify), and round-trip; evidence artifact in artifacts/section_10_5/bd-21z/.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyPond","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:46.140259819Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T17:29:09.808505224Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T17:29:09.808475899Z","close_reason":"Completed: signed receipt schema/sign/verify/export + CLI/spec/tests/evidence","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-5","self-contained","test-obligations"]}
 {"id":"bd-224kl","title":"Measure actual elapsed duration_ms in ShutdownReport instead of hardcoded 0 (connector/supervision.rs:820)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:32.604244191Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:21:14.073600555Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:21:14.073575839Z","close_reason":"Added Instant::now() at shutdown start, replaced duration_ms:0 with elapsed().as_millis() using u64::try_from().unwrap_or(u64::MAX).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":84,"issue_id":"bd-224kl","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"FILE: crates/franken-node/src/connector/supervision.rs around line 820
FIELD: duration_ms in ShutdownReport construction

CURRENT CODE:
  duration_ms: 0, // synchronous model; real impl would measure

PROBLEM: ShutdownReport.duration_ms is always 0. The shutdown process does take measurable time (there is drain logic, force-termination, event emission), but the elapsed time is never captured. This makes the duration metric useless for monitoring and capacity planning. Operators cannot tell if shutdowns are fast or slow.

REQUIRED FIX:
  1. Capture std::time::Instant::now() at the start of the shutdown sequence
  2. Compute elapsed = start.elapsed() before constructing ShutdownReport
  3. Set duration_ms: elapsed.as_millis() as u64 (or use saturating conversion)

CONSIDERATIONS:
- Check the shutdown function's scope to find where to place the Instant::now()
- The surrounding code may already use Instant for other timing — follow the same pattern
- Use u64::try_from(elapsed.as_millis()).unwrap_or(u64::MAX) for safe conversion (as_millis returns u128)
- Ensure the duration covers the entire shutdown: from initiation through drain through final cleanup
- This is a straightforward fix — no architectural changes needed
","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:56Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-229","title":"[PLAN 10.3] Migration System","description":"Section: 10.3 — Migration System

Strategic Context:
Migration autopilot pipeline from audit to rollout, designed to collapse migration friction with deterministic confidence and replayability.

Execution Requirements:
- Preserve all scoped capabilities from the canonical plan.
- Keep contracts self-contained so future contributors can execute without reopening the master plan.
- Require explicit testing strategy (unit + integration/e2e) and structured logging/telemetry for every child bead.
- Require artifact-backed validation for performance, security, and correctness claims.

Dependency Semantics:
- This epic is blocked by its child implementation beads.
- Cross-epic dependencies encode strategic sequencing and canonical ownership boundaries.

## Success Criteria
- All child beads for this section are completed with acceptance artifacts attached and no unresolved blockers.
- Section-level verification gate for comprehensive unit tests, integration/e2e workflows, and detailed structured logging evidence is green.
- Scope-to-plan traceability is explicit, with no feature loss versus the canonical plan section.

## Optimization Notes
- User-Outcome Lens: "[PLAN 10.3] Migration System" must improve operator confidence, safety posture, and deterministic recovery behavior under both normal and adversarial conditions.
- Cross-Section Coordination: child beads must encode integration assumptions explicitly to avoid local optimizations that degrade system-wide correctness.
- Verification Non-Negotiable: completion requires reproducible unit/integration/E2E evidence and structured logs suitable for independent replay.
- Scope Protection: any simplification must preserve canonical-plan feature intent and be justified with explicit tradeoff documentation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:40.464258670Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T08:14:25.733474385Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T08:14:25.733446132Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-229","depends_on_id":"bd-12f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:04Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-229","depends_on_id":"bd-2a0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:04Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-229","depends_on_id":"bd-2ew","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:04Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-229","depends_on_id":"bd-2st","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:04Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-229","depends_on_id":"bd-33x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:04Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-229","depends_on_id":"bd-3dn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:04Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-229","depends_on_id":"bd-3enl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:04Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-229","depends_on_id":"bd-3f9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:04Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-229","depends_on_id":"bd-3il","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:04Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-229","depends_on_id":"bd-cda","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:04Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-229","depends_on_id":"bd-hg1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:04Z","created_by":"import","meta  {"id":"bd-22e7","title":"[5] Method Stack Compliance — 4 mandatory execution disciplines","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md — Section 5

## Why This Exists
franken_node is driven by four complementary methodologies. These are not optional preferences; they are mandatory execution disciplines. Every implementation bead must comply with at least one of these method stacks, and cross-cutting beads must demonstrate compliance with all applicable stacks.

## Four Required Method Stacks

### 5.1 extreme-software-optimization (Execution Discipline)
Mandatory loop for every performance-sensitive change:
1. Baseline (p50/p95/p99, throughput, memory, cold start)
2. Profile (top hotspots only)
3. Prove behavior invariance and compatibility envelopes
4. Implement one lever
5. Verify compatibility/security artifacts
6. Re-profile
NO optimization lands without artifact-backed regression safety.

### 5.2 alien-artifact-coding (Mathematical Decision Core)
Use formal decision systems for product control surfaces:
- Expected-loss rollout choices
- Posterior trust state updates
- Confidence-aware migration recommendations
- Explainable policy decisions and receipts

### 5.3 alien-graveyard (High-EV Primitive Selection)
Adopt only high-EV disruptive primitives with fallback contracts:
- EV thresholding (EV >= 2.0)
- Failure-mode predesign
- Deterministic degraded operation pathways

### 5.4 porting-to-rust (Spec-First Essence Extraction Protocol)
For compatibility surfaces, apply the porting discipline as extraction-and-proof:
- Extract behavior into explicit specs (data shapes, invariants, defaults, errors, edge cases)
- Capture Node/Bun fixture outputs as conformance baselines
- Implement from spec and fixture contracts, NOT legacy source structure
- Enforce parity and divergence visibility via lockstep oracle + artifact gates
RULE: Legacy code is input to specification and oracle generation, NOT implementation blueprint.

## Compliance Mapping to Execution Tracks
- 5.1 (perf discipline): Required for 10.6, 10.14, 10.15, 10.17, 10.18
- 5.2 (math decision): Required for 10.5, 10.17, 10.19, 10.20, 10.21
- 5.3 (high-EV primitives): Required for all 10.x tracks adopting advanced primitives
- 5.4 (spec-first extraction): Required for 10.2, 10.3, 10.7

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each implementation PR cites which method stack(s) it follows
- Performance PRs include before/after baseline artifacts (5.1)
- Decision-surface PRs include formal decision rationale (5.2)
- New primitive adoption PRs include EV analysis and fallback contract (5.3)
- Compatibility PRs include spec reference and fixture IDs (5.4)

## Testing Requirements
- CI gate checking for method stack citation in PR descriptions
- Audit script validating artifact presence for performance changes
- Review checklist enforcing method stack compliance


## Additional Verification Requirements
- Unit tests for method-stack compliance validators (citation checks, artifact-rule checks, stack-to-change classification).
- E2E compliance scripts that execute representative change flows and verify required method-stack evidence is produced end-to-end.
- Structured logs for compliance evaluations with deterministic rule IDs, pass/fail status, and remediation guidance.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T16:14:34.097919725Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T21:02:44.888350975Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T21:02:44.888326650Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cross-cutting","methodology","plan","section-5"]}
 {"id":"bd-22lo","title":"CRDT conformance fixtures: update OR-Set tests for replica-aware add API","description":"Fresh-eyes audit on recent connector changes found that the OR-Set API in crates/franken-node/src/connector/crdt.rs now requires add(replica_id, element) after the causal-tag refactor, but tests/conformance/crdt_merge_fixtures.rs still calls the old single-argument form. That leaves the Rust conformance fixture surface out of sync with the implementation and can break all-target verification. Update the conformance fixtures to supply deterministic replica IDs while preserving commutativity, associativity, and idempotency intent, then validate with rch-targeted cargo checks/tests only.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"AzureCave","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:00:00.104768953Z","created_by":"AzureCave","updated_at":"2026-03-12T00:06:48.904047891Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T00:06:48.904021972Z","close_reason":"Updated tests/conformance/crdt_merge_fixtures.rs for replica-aware OrSet::add API; checker validation passed and attempted rch cargo target check confirmed this fixture is not a standalone Cargo test target.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
